title: Add local integration and system test targets
body:
These tests need to operate as part of the c/image repository CI to
verify downstream-usage.  That environment is already inside the
container built from the Dockerfile (here).  Support this use-case by
adding 'local' targets which bypass the container build.  Also,
simplify the "in-container" check to more specifically verify the exact
container image it's operating under.

Ref: https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1140

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body:
> Please make the in-container targets invoke the new -local ones

Sure thing, good idea.

> WRT the new comments

Okay, I can put more thought/time into better documenting/commenting these.  Another possibility is I could just drop the `Makefile` changes entirely and instead run the hack script directly from `.cirrus.yml` instead.  Somehow that seems less developer-friendly though.

WRT the comments, just a quick “this is for experts, don’t use if if you don’t know you are one” is the most important part to me, to decrease risk of accidents.

Given that, it would be perfectly fair to not bother with the details, or even drop the other comments instead of fine-tuning them :) After all, we have hardly have any documentation for `make test-integration` and `make test-system`.

> even drop the other comments

I agree, this is what I was working on just before you commented :smile:

(BTW of `.cirrus.yml`, `Makefile` and `hack/make.sh`, my very weak preference would be to get rid of `hack/make.sh`; it’s a vestigial part of a partial re-use of a much larger build system, probably obscuring more than helping. But clearly whoever is doing the work gets to make the decisions, and I’m not even here. This comment does not exist :) )
@mtrmac I'm also thinking I should try and rig something up to test the changes here, under c/image's `.cirrus.yml` script. My gut tells me this PR is overly simplistic.

Confirmed: These changes do allow the c/image CI to at least run the integration tests.  The system-tests currently fail due to a difficult-to-fix podman-in-docker conundrum.  I propose we fix that separately to allow the double travis->cirrus migrations to move forward.
Oof, we're also going to need a workaround for the [silly `toomanyrequests: You have reached your pull rate limit.` thing](https://travis-ci.org/github/containers/skopeo/jobs/761176748) :confounded:
@mtrmac PTAL, this one is ready to go.
